title: Setting a cell or row Protection Locked to true does not prevent editing in xlsx
author: ahouck

comments.author: FlorisVALETUDIE
comments.body: Any news on this issue ? I observed the same behaviour.

comments.author: Kelendria
comments.body:
After setting the protection information on a cell or a row and before writing the file you have to protect the worksheet.

```
sheet.protect('thepassword', {
          formatCells: true,
          formatColumns: true,
          formatRows: true,
          insertRows: true,
          insertColumns: false,
          insertHyperlinks: true,
          deleteRows: true,
          deleteColumns: false,
          sort: true,
          autoFilter: true
        })
```

comments.author: maqtulio
DavidBronfen
comments.body:
Can I just protect 2 specific columns and unprotect the rest of the sheet (including new columns/cells that will be added in the future by the user)?
Did anyone manage to do that?

Basically, I've protected the whole sheet and then unprotected the cells which should be editable (e.g. all the cells under columns C, D, E) the issue with this kind of workaround is that the user can't create new cells because the rest of the sheet is protected

comments.author: mcmule
comments.body:
I have the exact same issue : locking cells/rows doesn't do anything, even if protect() is called on the sheet. I also tried to protect() the sheet without using per cell/per row locking, and this does not do anything. 
Using v3.8.2 on Node 10 w. Typescript.

comments.author: FlorisVALETUDIE
comments.body: I kinda gave up on this issue when I realised it depends on your spreadsheet editor : protect worked when I used Microsoft excel, but it was ignored by every other editor (gSheet, openOffice, numbers, etc.).

comments.author: VikasAnand354
comments.body:
Any resolution on this issue?
I am facing the same issue

comments.body:
I have created an excel and i want to block the headers so that users cannot update the headers.
any other way if there is no update in this library.
